summary: AUDIT worker sweep — model/effort/rep parameterized; per-rep phase groups auto-create from the ACTUAL reps run (do NOT hardcode rep phases here, or the /workflows display implies more reps than execute).
logs: full-matrix mode: 2 reps × 1 models × 7 workers = 14 cells

glossary.claim: Single-field record in State/ChartEvaluation.cs returned by IChartCacheStore.EnsureAsync. Carries just BrightnessInputsChanged; consumed only by the coordinator's post-apply hook to gate PushSkyKSInputs(ctx).
glossary.finding: ChartEvaluation has three fields, not one. The coordinator reads EnsureWork and RenderWork directly in its pipeline code (not through the post-apply hook). The 'single-field' and 'consumed only by... post-apply hook' descriptions are both stale.
glossary.evidence: State/ChartEvaluation.cs:25-40 — record has required bool BrightnessInputsChanged, int EnsureWork, int RenderWork. State/ChartCoordinator.cs:212-213 — coordinator reads eval.EnsureWork and eval.RenderWork directly to compute renderProgress, before the post-apply hook is called at line 226.

glossary.location: CLAUDE.md §Glossary — NightDate bullet (line 46): DayWindowKey survivor claim
glossary.claim: DayWindowKey survives only inside ChartLayout.BuildDayWindow for the chart's render-time X-axis bounds and is carried as the entries' Window field.
glossary.finding: DayWindowKey is used in multiple places beyond ChartLayout.BuildDayWindow: as a member field in AltitudeSubChart_Day (mLastDayKey), as a local in AltitudeSubChart_Sky, and as a member field in MainForm.CheckboxTintPresenter (mLastAppliedDayKey). The 'only inside ChartLayout.BuildDayWindow' claim overstates its confinement.
glossary.evidence: Charts/AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:132 — private DayWindowKey mLastDayKey; Charts/AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:374 — DayWindowKey dayKey = dayWindow.Key; Charts/AltitudeSubChart_Sky.cs:318 — DayWindowKey dayKey = dayWindow.Key; Forms/Presenters/MainForm.CheckboxTintPresenter.cs:105 — private DayWindowKey mLastAppliedDayKey;

conventions.location: CLAUDE.md § "Conventions worth knowing before editing" → Defaults bullet: "apply **Pattern C** (fill any null/empty top-level field from the seed — additive-schema migration self-heal)"
conventions.claim: Pattern C fills any null/empty top-level field in the deserialised AppSettings from the seed (additive-schema migration self-heal).
conventions.finding: SettingsStore.Load only fills three specific fields — NinaTargetsRoot, ImageLibraryRoot, and NamedLocations — not every null/empty top-level field. LastSelectedLocationName is NOT filled by Pattern C. A developer adding a new AppSettings field and relying on this description would get no auto-fill without adding explicit code in SettingsStore.Load.
conventions.evidence: SettingsStore.cs:62-68 — three explicit null/empty checks only: `if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(settings.NinaTargetsRoot))`, `if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(settings.ImageLibraryRoot))`, `if (settings.NamedLocations == null || settings.NamedLocations.Count == 0)`. No check for LastSelectedLocationName.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md § Architecture, 'Cache state' bullet — '`(Location, Target)` yearDays'
architecture.claim: The yearDays axis is keyed by `(Location, Target)`.
architecture.finding: The actual `CacheAxis` key type is `Target` alone, not `(Location, Target)`. Location-scoping is implicit: `SetLocationAsync` drains and resets all four axes when the location changes, but the dictionary key stored in `mYearDaysAxis` is just `Target`. The code's own inline comment at line 57 also says 'yearDays is per-target'.
architecture.evidence: ChartCacheStore.cs:65 — `private readonly CacheAxis<Target, TargetCacheEntry> mYearDaysAxis;`; ChartCacheStore.cs:57 — '// yearDays is per-target'

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md § Architecture, 'Cache state' bullet — 'slice the night-spanning entries to `DayWindowKey.Range` at paint time'
architecture.claim: Sub-charts slice night-spanning cache entries to `DayWindowKey.Range` at paint time.
architecture.finding: `DayWindowKey` has no `.Range` property; it exposes only `ChartStartUtcTicks`, `Count`, and the derived `ChartStartUtc`. Year and Sessions sub-charts read the full `YearDays` and `Nights` arrays directly from `GetOrNull`/`GetFitOrNull` — there is no DayWindowKey-based slicing at paint time. The trajectory entry (`TargetTrajectoryEntry`) is already pre-keyed per `NightDate` and its `Samples` are read in full.
architecture.evidence: DayWindowKey.cs:26-46 (no Range member); AltitudeSubChart_Year.cs:257-260 (`cache.GetOrNull(target)` → `yearEntry.YearDays`, no DayWindowKey slicing); AltitudeSubChart_Sessions.cs:269-274 (same pattern)

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md § Architecture, Threading paragraph — 'cache pre-population is **2–4 sec for 44 targets** (yearDays)'
architecture.claim: YearDays pre-population for 44 targets takes 2–4 seconds.
architecture.finding: The code's own cancellation-policy remark in `ChartCacheStore` states '~1-2 sec for 44 targets post-CS-removal' for the same yearDays build. The two estimates are in direct conflict (1–2 sec in code vs. 2–4 sec in doc).
architecture.evidence: ChartCacheStore.cs:33-35 — 'Compute is short (~1-2 sec for 44 targets post-CS-removal); the wasted CPU is bounded…'

architecture.claim: RenderArea executes exactly three steps: Render → ShowOnly → Refresh.
architecture.finding: The actual implementation has four steps: `sc.Render(ctx, mCache, progress)` → `ShowOnlyAltitudeChart(sc.Control)` → `ResizeAltitudeChartArea(sc.IdealHeight)` → `sc.Control.Refresh()`. The `ResizeAltitudeChartArea` call between ShowOnly and Refresh is absent from the doc. The doc also omits the `progress` argument on `Render`, though that parameter is optional so it is valid syntax.
architecture.evidence: MainForm.ChartBuildPresenter.cs:241-255 — sc.Render(ctx, mCache, progress); ShowOnlyAltitudeChart(sc.Control); ResizeAltitudeChartArea(sc.IdealHeight); sc.Control.Refresh();

deps-build.claim: The WinExe csproj has exactly two ProjectReference entries: Astronomy.Core and Astronomy.NINA
deps-build.finding: The csproj has a third ProjectReference: Astronomy.Diagnostics (`..\..\Library\Astronomy.Diagnostics\Astronomy.Diagnostics.csproj`). This dependency is absent from the 'easy to miss' list, which is the section specifically intended to call out non-obvious sibling deps.

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md § Tool preference for symbol queries (line 64): "one project authored here plus two sibling-library projects listed for Solution Explorer visibility"
deps-build.claim: The solution contains one authored project and two sibling-library projects for Solution Explorer visibility
deps-build.finding: The solution contains two authored projects (TargetPlanner and TargetPlanner.Tests) and three sibling-library projects (Astronomy.Core, Astronomy.XISF, Astronomy.NINA). The count is wrong on both sides.

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md § External dependencies that are easy to miss (lines 73-74): "MainForm.Button_BrowseTargetList_Click now uses the stock FolderBrowserDialog (single-folder only)"
deps-build.claim: After LocalLib was dropped, Button_BrowseTargetList_Click uses FolderBrowserDialog for single-folder picking
deps-build.finding: Button_BrowseTargetList_Click calls GetBrowsedTargets() → PromptForFilesOrFolder(), which uses a multi-select OpenFileDialog (with a folder-open trick: a single non-existent sentinel filename is interpreted as the containing directory). FolderBrowserDialog is only used in PromptForFolder(), which is the fallback path for the Load Image Library / Load JSON Targets buttons when the configured root yields nothing — not for the Browse button.
deps-build.evidence: TargetPlanner/Forms/Presenters/MainForm.TargetLoadingPresenter.cs:50-54 (Button_BrowseTargetList_Click → GetBrowsedTargets); :76-82 (GetBrowsedTargets → PromptForFilesOrFolder); :277-302 (PromptForFilesOrFolder uses OpenFileDialog); :306-318 (PromptForFolder uses FolderBrowserDialog, called from Load buttons' fallback path)

deps-build.location: CLAUDE.md §§ Build / run (line 54-58) and Tool preference for symbol queries (lines 64-68)
deps-build.claim: The '## Build / run' section contains csproj-level gotchas and build bullet content
deps-build.finding: The '## Build / run' heading (line 54) holds only a one-sentence redirector to VERIFICATION.md. The actual build bullets — solution structure, TFM, configurations, build commands, test-project invocation, and scoping rationale — are placed as sub-bullets under the '## Tool preference for symbol queries' heading (line 64-68). Per the heading's own preamble ('The csproj-level gotchas and test-project scoping rationale below are kept here'), these bullets were intended to follow the Build/run section but now fall under the wrong heading, mixing build guidance into a symbol-query-tool section.
deps-build.evidence: CLAUDE.md:54-58 (## Build / run — empty except for redirector), CLAUDE.md:60-68 (## Tool preference for symbol queries — contains build bullets starting at line 64)

core-contract.claim: The 'Core consumer contract' section lives in the CLAUDE.md router and carries four detailed bullets documenting Astronomy.Core's API guarantees (IReadOnlyList return convention, DateTime.Kind/TimeKindGuard behavior, signed-degree parameter contract, no static mutable state).
core-contract.finding: CLAUDE.md's charter is 'always-loaded router + load-bearing gotchas, kept thin.' A dedicated four-bullet API-contract block for the sibling library exceeds the router charter. ARCHITECTURE.md already owns the Core surface summary (§Astronomy.Core — pure math, no UI, lines 7-25) and cross-references back to CLAUDE.md for this section (ARCHITECTURE.md line 3: 'for…the Core consumer contract, see CLAUDE.md'; line 13: 'See the Core consumer contract section in CLAUDE.md for the Kind=Utc rationale'). The split forces a reader editing Core API to consult both files: Core surface types in ARCHITECTURE.md, API contracts about those same types in CLAUDE.md. Consolidating into ARCHITECTURE.md's existing Core surface section and replacing the CLAUDE.md content with a single cross-ref pointer would end the mutual-reference loop and keep the router thin.
core-contract.evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md line 3 and line 13 confirm ARCHITECTURE.md defers to CLAUDE.md for Core contract content, creating a circular split. ARCHITECTURE.md lines 7-25 already document the same Core surface (NightWindow kinds at line 13, signed-degree TargetGeometry at line 16) — the contract rationale in CLAUDE.md is an addendum to that existing section, not a router gotcha.

core-contract.location: CLAUDE.md §Core consumer contract — all four bullets (lines 113-117)
core-contract.claim: Four present-tense factual claims about Astronomy.Core: (1) Session/Moon helpers (VisibilityWindows.For, QualitySamples.OverNight, MoonSeparation.IntervalsAboveDeg, BestSession-style planners) return IReadOnlyList<T>; (2) NightWindow.AstronomicalDawn/AstronomicalDusk are DateTimeKind.Utc and Core routes DateTime inputs through TimeKindGuard.AsUtc; (3) TargetGeometry.MeridianAltitude and related helpers take signed lat/dec degrees; (4) Core has no static mutable state.
core-contract.finding: All four claims depend on Astronomy.Core source code in the sibling Library repo. The Library is absent from this fixture. Two claims have indirect corroboration from ARCHITECTURE.md (AstronomicalDawn/AstronomicalDusk Kind=Utc at line 13; TargetGeometry signed-degrees at line 16), and TP-side CoordinateExtensions.cs confirms callers resolve hemisphere flags before passing to Core (consistent with claim 3). The IReadOnlyList return-type claim and the TimeKindGuard routing detail cannot be confirmed or refuted from accessible source.
core-contract.evidence: unverifiable → ask user. Library source not present under E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\; all .cs files are under TargetPlanner\ only. ARCHITECTURE.md lines 13 and 16 provide partial indirect support for claims 2 and 3; claims 1 and the TimeKindGuard detail of claim 2 have no readable citation.

Only 2026-05-17-architecture-review.md and 2026-05-26-code-review-async-ui.md follow the stated YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md pattern. The parenthetical reads as a factual naming description, not merely a forward-looking convention.

crossref.claim: Item 5 (closed) links to `[docs/code-quality-audit.md](docs/code-quality-audit.md)` for per-item details of the 2026-05-19 audit.
crossref.finding: Rename-orphan: the target file is `docs/2026-05-19-code-quality-audit.md` (confirmed in directory listing), not `docs/code-quality-audit.md`. The link is broken — the date-prefixed filename was used when the file was created but the link omits the date prefix.

crossref.claim: K-S Sky brightness section links to `README.md#sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode` for user-facing UX.
crossref.finding: Dangling anchor: `README.md` has no heading `## Sky brightness — Day / Sky sub-mode` or similar. The actual heading is `## Sky brightness` (README.md:98), which produces anchor `#sky-brightness`. The link resolves to the README file but the anchor fragment is wrong and will not scroll to the section.
crossref.evidence: Grep `^## Sky brightness` in README.md matches line 98 as `## Sky brightness` only. No heading containing `day-sky-sub-mode` exists anywhere in README.md.

glossary.claim: "single-field record … Carries just BrightnessInputsChanged; consumed only by the coordinator's post-apply hook to gate PushSkyKSInputs(ctx)"
glossary.finding: ChartEvaluation has three fields, not one: BrightnessInputsChanged (required bool), EnsureWork (int), and RenderWork (int). Both EnsureWork and RenderWork are consumed inside RunPipelineAsync — before the post-apply hook — to compute progress offsets and to decide whether to surface the progress bar. The 'consumed only by the coordinator's post-apply hook' claim is therefore also false.
glossary.evidence: State/ChartEvaluation.cs:33-40 (EnsureWork and RenderWork fields with XML docs); State/ChartCoordinator.cs:212-213 (eval.EnsureWork and eval.RenderWork consumed in RunPipelineAsync for OffsetProgress construction, prior to the mPostApplyHook?.Invoke call at line 226)

conventions.location: CLAUDE.md §"Conventions worth knowing before editing" — Log.Diag bullet: "Current categories: `Coord`, `Cache`, `Day`, `Sky`, `UI`, `Overlay`"
conventions.claim: `Sky` is listed as a current Log.Diag category.
conventions.finding: There are zero `Log.Diag("Sky"` calls anywhere in `TargetPlanner/` — the category is not in use. All other listed categories (`Coord`, `Cache`, `Day`, `UI`, `Overlay`) have confirmed callers.
conventions.evidence: Grep `Log.Diag("Sky"` across `TargetPlanner/` (all .cs): no matches. Confirmed callers: `ChartCoordinator.cs:196` (Coord), `ChartCacheStore.cs:192,305,529` (Cache), `AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:348,495,505` (Day), `AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:210` (Overlay), many UI files (UI).

conventions.location: CLAUDE.md §"Conventions worth knowing before editing" — defaults/settings bullet: "apply Pattern C (fill any null/empty top-level field from the seed — additive-schema migration self-heal)"
conventions.claim: Pattern C fills any null/empty top-level field from the seed.
conventions.finding: `SettingsStore.Load` only Pattern-C fills three specific fields: `NinaTargetsRoot` (line 62), `ImageLibraryRoot` (line 65), and `NamedLocations` (line 66). `LastSelectedLocationName` — the boot-pick field explicitly mentioned in the same bullet — is NOT Pattern-C filled. The "any null/empty top-level field" description overstates the implementation.
conventions.evidence: `TargetPlanner/Settings/SettingsStore.cs:55-78` — Pattern C block fills only `NinaTargetsRoot`, `ImageLibraryRoot`, and `NamedLocations`. `LastSelectedLocationName` (field exists at `AppSettings.cs:26`) has no Pattern C guard in `SettingsStore.Load`.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md §Architecture, Cache state bullet (line 81): '`(Location, Target)` yearDays'
architecture.claim: The yearDays cache axis is keyed by `(Location, Target)`.
architecture.finding: The actual C# axis is `CacheAxis<Target, TargetCacheEntry>`, keyed by `Target` only — not `(Location, Target)`. Location-scoping is achieved by `SetLocationAsync` calling `DrainAndReset()` on all axes when the location changes; location is NOT part of the composite key. The other three axes' key descriptions in the same bullet (`(Target, HdmKey)`, `(Target, NightDate)`, `NightDate`) all match their actual C# types exactly.
architecture.evidence: ChartCacheStore.cs:65: `private readonly CacheAxis<Target, TargetCacheEntry> mYearDaysAxis;` — key type is `Target`, not `(Location, Target)`.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md §Architecture, Cache state bullet (line 81): 'Sub-charts read via sync `Get*OrNull` and slice the night-spanning entries to `DayWindowKey.Range` at paint time.'
architecture.claim: `DayWindowKey` exposes a `.Range` property that sub-charts use to slice night-spanning trajectory/moon entries at paint time.
architecture.finding: Two simultaneous errors. (1) `DayWindowKey` has no `.Range` property — the struct exposes only `ChartStartUtcTicks` (long), `Count` (int), and `ChartStartUtc` (computed DateTime). (2) The trajectory and moon entries are NOT night-spanning: both `BuildTrajectoryAsync` and `BuildMoonEphemerisAsync` pre-slice samples to the chart-visible window at cache BUILD time. AltitudeSubChart_Day reads `trajEntry.Samples` directly without any slicing.
architecture.evidence: DayWindowKey.cs:26-46 (fields: ChartStartUtcTicks, Count, ChartStartUtc — no Range). AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:445-447: `TargetTrajectoryEntry trajEntry = cache?.GetTrajectoryOrNull(target, nightDate); ... IReadOnlyList<AltAzSample> samples = trajEntry.Samples;` — direct read, no slice.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md §Architecture, Threading paragraph (line 85): 'Form-lifecycle cancellation is the one CTS (`mFormClosingCts` on MainForm), observed only at the outer `Task.WhenAny(warmupTask, cancelledTask)` boundary in the NINA-load warmup.'
architecture.claim: `mFormClosingCts` is consumed only at a single `Task.WhenAny` boundary inside the NINA-load warmup path.
architecture.finding: Two errors. (1) The token is also passed directly (not via WhenAny) as `mFormClosingCts.Token` to `TargetScanner.ScanAsync` for all scan operations, not just NINA loads. (2) The WhenAny boundary is inside `StartCacheWarmup`, which is shared by ALL four target-load entry points (startup image-library auto-load, Button_LoadImageLibrary, Button_BrowseTargetList, drag-and-drop) — not only the NINA path.
architecture.evidence: TargetLoadingPresenter.cs:247: `await TargetScanner.ScanAsync(path, kinds, mFormClosingCts.Token, progress)` (direct CT, all scan paths). TargetLoadingPresenter.cs:335-347: `CancellationToken formCt = mFormClosingCts.Token; ... Task.WhenAny(warmup, cancelled)` inside `StartCacheWarmup`, which is called from every load path.

architecture.claim: The `RenderArea` method performs exactly three steps: Render, ShowOnlyAltitudeChart, then Refresh.
architecture.finding: The actual sequence has four steps: `sc.Render(ctx, mCache, progress)` → `ShowOnlyAltitudeChart(sc.Control)` → `ResizeAltitudeChartArea(sc.IdealHeight)` → `sc.Control.Refresh()`. The `ResizeAltitudeChartArea(sc.IdealHeight)` call between ShowOnlyAltitudeChart and Refresh is absent from the description. The `progress` parameter to `sc.Render` is also elided.
architecture.evidence: MainForm.ChartBuildPresenter.cs:241-255: lines 241-255 show the full four-call sequence.

architecture.location: CLAUDE.md §Architecture, Threading paragraph (line 85): 'cache pre-population is **2–4 sec for 44 targets** (yearDays)'
architecture.claim: yearDays cache pre-population takes 2–4 seconds for 44 targets.
architecture.finding: Both IChartCacheStore.cs and ChartCacheStore.cs internal comments state the figure as '~1-2 sec for 44 targets' for the same yearDays computation. The doc's '2–4 sec' is approximately double the code-comment measurement.
architecture.evidence: IChartCacheStore.cs:36-37: 'Compute is short (~1-2 sec for 44 targets); the wasted CPU is bounded and acceptable'. ChartCacheStore.cs:34: 'Compute is short (~1-2 sec for 44 targets post-CS-removal)'.

core-contract.claim: Detailed Core library API contracts (IReadOnlyList<T> return convention, DateTime.Kind/TimeKindGuard.AsUtc, signed-degree inputs, no-static-mutable-state in Core) are carried in the always-loaded CLAUDE.md router
core-contract.finding: DOMAIN.md is explicitly chartered as the home for astronomy/coordinate/observing conventions and its Status section names this exact CLAUDE.md section as a live-but-pending-graduation source. The CLAUDE.md router is meant to stay thin (routing + load-bearing TP-side gotchas); a full Core API contract for the sibling library is domain content, not router content. ARCHITECTURE.md already cross-references the section ('See the Core consumer contract section in CLAUDE.md'), confirming it is treated as domain documentation rather than navigation scaffolding.
core-contract.evidence: DOMAIN.md:15–19: "CLAUDE.md §'Core consumer contract' — the DateTime.Kind UTC contract, signed-degree inputs, IReadOnlyList<T> return contract, no-static-mutable-state" listed under 'live sources' awaiting graduation. DOMAIN.md:22: 'When you next touch a domain convention, graduate its canonical statement here and leave a one-line pointer at the old location.' ARCHITECTURE.md:13: 'See the Core consumer contract section in CLAUDE.md for the Kind=Utc rationale.'

core-contract.location: CLAUDE.md §'Core consumer contract', signed-degree inputs bullet (line 115–116)
core-contract.claim: 'AltAz.At' is named as the canonical signed-degree resolution idiom
core-contract.finding: The static helper class is AltAzCalculator, not AltAz. AltAz is the readonly struct return type. The method signature is AltAzCalculator.At(target, location, utc). The doc conflates the return-type name with the calling class, producing an incorrect symbol reference for any agent following the guidance.
core-contract.evidence: AltitudeSubChart_Sky.cs:509: 'AltAz t = AltAzCalculator.At(target, location, utc);'; ChartCacheStore.cs:669: 'entry.AltDusk = AltAzCalculator.At(target, location, entry.Dusk).Altitude;'; ARCHITECTURE.md (Astronomy.Core surface, AltAz.cs entry): 'AltAzCalculator.At(target, location, utc) and AltAzCalculator.Of(target, location)...Returns an AltAz readonly struct'

crossref.claim: Closed item 5 references `[docs/code-quality-audit.md](docs/code-quality-audit.md)` for per-item details.
crossref.finding: The file `docs/code-quality-audit.md` does not exist. The actual file is `docs/2026-05-19-code-quality-audit.md` (the dated slug form). Link is dangling.

crossref.claim: Code comment references `docs/code-quality-audit.md` as the audit source for the extraction.
crossref.finding: Same broken path as ROADMAP.md — the actual file is `docs/2026-05-19-code-quality-audit.md`. The code comment carries a dangling doc reference.
crossref.evidence: `ls E:/Projects/AI/TargetPlanner/docs/` confirms no `code-quality-audit.md`. Grep for `docs/code-quality-audit` matches both `ROADMAP.md:24` and `TargetPlanner\Charts\FitTooltipResolver.cs:16`.

crossref.finding: `docs/screenshot.png` does not exist. The `docs/` directory contains only `2026-05-19-code-quality-audit.md`, `archive/`, `design/`, and `reference-material/`. The image link is dangling and will render as a broken image on GitHub.
crossref.evidence: `ls E:/Projects/AI/TargetPlanner/docs/` output does not include `screenshot.png`. No `*.png` found under `docs/` via glob.

crossref.claim: User-facing UX for K-S sky brightness is documented in `[README.md](README.md#sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode)`.
crossref.finding: The anchor `#sky-brightness-day-sky-sub-mode` does not exist in README.md. The README's sky-brightness section heading is `## Sky brightness` (anchor `#sky-brightness`). The link navigates to the page top rather than the intended section.
crossref.evidence: Grep of README.md headings shows `## Sky brightness` at line 98. No heading contains `day-sky-sub-mode`. The correct anchor is `#sky-brightness`.
